desc: 2-1-1 Oklahoma offers information and referral across the spectrum of human need including, but not limited to, food pantries, affordable housing, health resources, child care, after-school programs, caregiver support, financial programs, literacy, and job programs.

name: Legal Aid Services of Oklahoma
desc: If you’re worried about being evicted or already in the process of eviction, contact Legal Aid Services of Oklahoma for assistance.

name: PCs for People
desc: At PCs for People, individuals and families that meet eligibility requirements can receive a computer, computer repair, internet service and learning resources.

name: Regional Food Bank of Oklahoma
address: 3355 S Purdue Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73179
desc: The Regional Food Bank not only distributes food, it is a leader in educating the public on hunger, advocating for those living with hunger and providing access to additional resources.

name: Project 66
desc: At Project 66, we are committed to making a difference in the lives of individuals and families facing food insecurity. Through our programs, we strive to create a sustainable impact and bring hope to those in need.

name: Food Bank of Eastern Oklahoma
desc: There is enough food in our community for everyone. With the right resources, hunger is a problem we can solve together.

name: Metropolitan Library System
desc: The Metropoolitan Library System is a public librarysystem consisting of 19 libraries that serves Oklahoma County, Oklahoma.

name: Eastern Oklahoma Library System
desc: The Eastern Oklahoma Library System is a multi-county organization providing public library service through fifteen libraries in six counties.

name: Pioneer Library System
desc: The Pioneer Library System serves the residents of Cleveland, McClain, and Pottawatomie Counties through twelve branches in 10 communities and Information Stations in rural communities.

name: Southeast Oklahoma Library System
desc: The Southeast Oklahoma Library System strives to be the place where people explore yesterday's heritage, today's information, and tomorrow's dreams.

name: Southern Oklahoma Library System
address: Southern Oklahoma Library System, 601 Railway Express, Ardmore, OK 73401
desc: The Southern Oklahoma Library System serves over 97,000 citizens across five counties with eight libraries located in Ardmore, Atoka, Davis, Healdton, Marietta, Sulphur, Tishomingo and Wilson.

name: Southern Prairie Library System
desc: To meet the informational needs of all ages, the Southern Prairie Library System commits its resources to the promotion of reading and lifelong learning.

name: Tulsa City-County Library
desc: The Tulsa City-County Library strives to promote lifelong learning and literacy in all forms.  Learn more about the goals, vision, and core values of the Library.

name: Western Plains Library System
area: Custer, Dewey, Roger Mills, and Washita Counties
desc: Western Plains Library System strives to maintain a welcoming and safe environment.

name: Oklahoma Library of Digital Resources
desc: The resources within the Oklahoma Library of Digital Resources are high-quality digital content vetted by Oklahoma teachers and support Oklahoma Academic Standards.

name: Open Learn OK
desc: Open educational resources (OER) will equip the Oklahoma State System for Higher Education and Oklahoma institutions to create opportunities for diverse, equal, and collaborative knowledge creation and dissemination for students through no-cost, openly-licensed materials.

name: Ambassador House
desc: Transitional living for women and children only.

name: Catholic Charities Archdiocese of OKC - Holy Family Home
address: 1232 N. Classen Blvd. Oklahoma City, OK
desc: Assists homeless women and families in transitioning into permanent housing.

name: Catholic Charities Archdiocese of OKC - Sanctuary Women's Development Center
desc: Day shelter serving women age 18+. Provides social services to homeless and at-risk women and their children including employment resources and resume building, trauma counseling, nutritious beverages and snacks, community food pantry and clothing closet, and a safe place to rest and refocus.

name: City Care - Pershing Center
address: 2400 General Pershing Blvd. Oklahoma City, OK 73107
desc: Provides food and shelter for the homeless and housing for low-income families.

name: City Rescue Mission
desc: Emergency shelter for homeless men, women, and families, and provides crisis counseling, clothing, rehab programs, and meals.

name: Compassionate Hands
desc: Referrals to community resources, utility assistance, and housing and emergency shelter for zip code 73099.

name: Family Function
desc: Temporary, emergency youth shelter for children ages 12-17.

name: Food and Shelter, Inc.
desc: Food, housing, and emergency services for people in crisis.

name: Grace Rescue Mission
desc: Shelter housing for men only (min. age of 19 with current photo ID).

name: Hope Community Services, Inc.
desc: Community living programs to assist homeless, mentally ill adults. Serving Cleveland, Canadian, and Oklahoma county residents.

name: Jesus House
address: 1335 W Sheridan Ave. Oklahoma City, OK 73106
desc: Emergency shelter for single men and women age 18+. Provides clothing, meals, and other assistance.

name: Mid-Del Youth Shelter
address: 2600 N Air Depot, Midwest City, OK 73141
desc: Emergency youth shelter (open 24 hours, 7 days a week) for children and adolescents (ages 0-17).

name: Neighborhood Services Organization (NSO)
desc: Transitional housing programs for single men (ages 18-23), homeless families with multiple children, homeless, pregnant young women (ages 18-23), and women caring for a child under 5

name: Oklahoma City Housing Authority
desc: Help with resume writing and budgeting skills.

name: Salvation Army of Central OK
desc: Transitional housing, emergency shelter for men, and emergency family and women’s shelter. Serves Oklahoma, Canadian, and Cleveland counties.

name: Salvation Army Adult Rehabilitation Center
desc: Rehabilitation work therapy program for adult male substance abusers age 21+.

name: Sisu Youth, Inc.
address: 1225 Sovereign Row, Ste. 105, Oklahoma City, OK 73108
desc: Day shelter connecting metro teens with support for basic needs like food, clothing, and personal care items. For older teens under age 18.

name: Sunbeam Family Services
desc: Emergency and contract foster care for abused/ neglected children, individual, marital, and family counseling, and senior services.

name: Transition House, Inc.
desc: Transitional living programs and drop-in center.

name: Upward Transitions
address: 1134 W Main St. Oklahoma City, OK 73106
desc: Provides assistance to families and individuals who face threats to financial stability or who are homeless or on the verge of becoming homeless.

name: Westtown Homeless Resource and Day Center
desc: Provides housing and supportive services to homeless and at-risk families, including meals, health clinic, computers, showers, and on-site social services.

name: Myriad Botanical Gardens
desc: Myriad Botanical Gardens seeks to increase the quality of life and vibrancy of downtown Oklahoma City by sustaining a world-class public garden and community-building urban green space. The Gardens offers a diverse range of engaging horticultural, cultural and recreational programs, exhibits and events for all ages to learn, grow and thrive.

name: Robbers Cave State Park
desc: This park is a great example of Parkitecture, the National Park Service’s rustic design style, seen in features like hand-cut metal signs and cabins that blend into the landscape with native stone and live-edge cedar siding. With its lakes, hills, woods, and trails, Robbers Cave offers a rich variety of scenery and activities for families to explore and enjoy.

name: Beavers Bend State Park
desc: Beavers Bend State Park is located in the mountainous region of southeast Oklahoma along the shores of Broken Bow Lake and the Mountain Fork River. Guests traveling down the winding roads through the forests of pine and hardwood trees will find adventure, beautiful scenery and plenty of activities inside this state park. The scenic beauty of Beavers Bend State Park makes it one of Oklahoma's most popular areas.

name: Scissortail Park
desc: located south of Myriad Botanical Gardens, it continues south close to the shore of the Oklahoma River. Featuring a variety of engaging experiences for visitors including ornamental gardens and woodlands, a 3.7 acre lake, children’s playground and nature play area, interactive water fountains, seasonal roller rink, dog park, sports courts, picnic grove, restaurant, and performance stage and great lawn, the Park has something for everyone.

name: Wheeler Park
desc: Visitors can enjoy award-winning restaurants and breweries, locally owned artisan shops, lively events, and the iconic Ferris Wheel—all part of Wheeler’s long-term vision for a dynamic, connected community.

name: Wiley Post Park
desc: Wiley Post Park sits right along the Oklahoma River and was named after the famous Oklahoma pilot that circumnavigated the globe in 1931. The park also offers river trails, open fields, a popular playground, splashpad, and an outdoor pavilion with its very own sand volleyball court. The park is just across the street from the Mat Hoffman Action Sports park, the biggest skate park in the city.

name: Little Sahara State Park
desc: A prime example of Oklahoma's diverse terrain, Little Sahara State Park boasts over 1,600 acres of sand dunes, ranging in height from 25 to 75 feet. The vast dunes have formed over time from terrace deposits, remnants of prehistoric times when the Cimarron River flowed over the entire area. Located south of Waynoka in northwest Oklahoma, the park offers RV sites with water, electric and sewer hookups, tent sites, picnic areas and comfort stations

name: Lake Murray State Park
desc: Lake Murray State Park is Oklahoma's first and largest state park consisting of 12,500 acres of forested, rolling hills around beautiful Lake Murray. The park's diverse terrain, exceptional trails and historic sites make Lake Murray State Park a favorite destination among outdoor, water sport and ATV riding enthusiasts.

name: Mitch Park
desc: This is the largest city park in Edmond located between North Santa Fe and North Kelly along West Covell Road. Mitch park includes 240 acres of leisure and recreational space. There are sports fields, public facility building where a weekend farmers market is held. Public toilets and other facilities and a couple different parking areas.

name: Natural Falls State Park
desc: Located near the Oklahoma/Arkansas border in the scenic Ozark Highlands region of northeast Oklahoma, Natural Falls State Park features a stunning 77-foot waterfall cascading through rock formations and creating a hidden, serene atmosphere at the bottom of a narrow V-shaped valley. A railed observation platform allows hikers to overlook the falls and another observation deck with seating is located at the foot of the falls offering two vantage points to enjoy one of the most scenic wonders in the state.
